votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the recursive logic using an iterative approach, maintaining the same parity checks for indices and values.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic, summing the even elements that are located at odd indices.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic using an iterative approach with enumerate, accurately checking for odd indices and even values to compute the sum.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic. In Coq, sum_even_at_odd_from starts at index i and increments by 1 for each element, checking if the current index is odd and the element is even. The Python version uses enumerate starting from 0 and computes the actual index as (i + idx), which gives the same sequence of indices. Both check for odd index ((i + idx) % 2 != 0 in Python, Nat.odd i in Coq) and even element (x % 2 == 0 in Python, Z.even x in Coq). The add_spec function correctly checks equality of s with the computed sum.

The Python code correctly implements the same logic as the Coq code. In Coq, sum_even_at_odd_from starts at index i and increments by 1 for each element, checking if the current index is odd and the element is even. The Python version uses enumerate starting from 0 and adds i to get the effective index (i + idx), which is equivalent. Both check for odd index (% 2 != 0 in Python, Nat.odd in Coq) and even element (% 2 == 0 in Python, Z.even in Coq). The add_spec function correctly checks equality of s with the computed sum.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic. In Coq, sum_even_at_odd_from starts at index i and increments with each recursive call, checking if the current index is odd and the element is even. The Python version uses enumerate starting from 0 and computes the effective index as (i + idx), which matches the Coq behavior where the index starts at i and increments. Both check for odd index ((i + idx) % 2 != 0 in Python, Nat.odd i in Coq) and even element (x % 2 == 0 in Python, Z.even x in Coq). The add_spec function correctly checks equality of s with the computed sum.
Both implementations sum elements x where x is even and the index (i+position) is odd; Python mirrors Coq’s parity checks and accumulation, and add_spec checks the same equality.
Both sum even-valued elements at odd indices offset by i (starting at 0), and add_spec checks equality with that sum; Python’s modulo/parity matches Coq’s Z.even for all integers.

Both sum_even_at_odd_from and sum_even_at_odd sum elements that are even and at odd indices (with 0-based starting i), and add_spec checks equality with that sum. Parity and negatives are handled consistently in Python and Coq.
